title: Head of Operations and Revenue
description: Part of the ScraperAPI team at saas.group, the Head of Operations & Revenue owns the systems, processes, and operating cadence that allow a remote SaaS business to scale efficiently. Requires 8+ years in operations, revenue operations, business operations, or general management within B2B SaaS, plus strong hands-on experience with data, metrics, BI dashboards, and operational reporting. Works closely with the CEO, integrating operational and data foundations across merged businesses in a fully remote, distributed organization.
score_reason: Strong match on seniority (15+ years vs 8+ required), RevOps/GTM expertise, and B2B SaaS background. Remote global role aligns perfectly with location flexibility. Only minor gap: role emphasizes general business operations and BI/metrics reporting over specific GTM motion ownership.
ai_opener: I came across saas.group's Head of Operations & Revenue role and it resonates with my background scaling RevOps and GTM systems across multiple B2B SaaS businesses. Having spent 15+ years building the operational and data foundations that enable remote, distributed teams to scale efficiently—including pipeline forecasting, metrics architecture, and cross-functional cadences—I'm particularly interested in how you're integrating operations across merged businesses under the ScraperAPI umbrella.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: As Revenue Operations Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations, you'll accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy and scalable territory design. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Strategy, or GTM planning, deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, sales territories and segmentation, plus strong analytical skills with Excel, SQL, and BI tools. Maintains territory mapping in CRM systems like Salesforce and Fullcast while leading market expansion and rep capacity modeling projects.
score_reason: Exceptional match: Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps directly aligns with Gong's need for territory strategy expertise, Salesforce proficiency, and data-driven GTM planning. The role's focus on pipeline forecasting, seller productivity, and scalable operations is core to his background.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I noticed Gong is building out Territory Strategy & Operations—an area I've spent the last 15 years optimizing for high-growth B2B SaaS companies. I've designed and scaled territory models that directly drove rep productivity and pipeline predictability, and I'm excited about the opportunity to bring that expertise to an org like Gong that's clearly prioritizing data-driven revenue architecture.

title: RevOps Manager
description: Administer the CRM (HubSpot) and RevOps tools, ensuring integrations, data hygiene, and system reliability while partnering with sales, marketing, and finance on goals and revenue strategies. Requires 3+ years in revenue operations, sales operations, or business analytics, preferably in tech (SaaS, cloud, AI). Supports forecasting, territory planning, and quota setting, with familiarity in KPIs like CAC, LTV, churn, and sales cycle length in a healthcare-focused, remote European team.
score_reason: Excellent match: 15+ years B2B SaaS experience exceeds 3+ year requirement, HubSpot admin expertise aligns perfectly, deep CAC/LTV/forecasting knowledge directly applicable, and remote European option accommodates location flexibility.
ai_opener: I noticed Platomics is building RevOps infrastructure for a healthcare AI team across Europe—that's exactly where I've spent the last 15 years. I've scaled HubSpot-first GTM operations in fast-growth SaaS environments, from pipeline forecasting to territory planning, and I'm particularly drawn to how healthcare companies need bulletproof data hygiene and compliance-first systems. I'd love to explore how my experience could help you build a revenue operations function that scales with your European team.

title: GTM Engineer
description: At this B2B SaaS facility management company, the GTM Engineer helps build, configure, and continuously improve intelligent AI agents that help Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success work smarter and faster. You'll work at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, designing how their 'AI brain' learns and operates in the real world. The role focuses on both how AI systems connect and run, and how they learn and improve over time as part of their first AI Workforce.
score_reason: Strong alignment with AI-native GTM focus and RevOps automation expertise, but role emphasizes AI agent engineering/configuration which leans more technical than traditional RevOps leadership. Remote location is ideal fit.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines in B2B SaaS, and I'm genuinely intrigued by FacilityOS's approach to embedding AI agents directly into Sales and Marketing workflows. The intersection of data-driven automation and human intelligence is exactly where I've seen the highest ROI, and I'd love to explore how your 'AI brain' learns and improves from real-world GTM operations.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Test, implement and optimize an AI outbound solution from setup through performance tuning, build automated outbound sequences, and own email marketing infrastructure including deliverability monitoring. Requires 3+ years in marketing operations or GTM engineering/growth engineer roles at B2B SaaS companies, hands-on experience with marketing automation tools, and strong technical skills with APIs and webhooks. Collaborates with RevOps on lead scoring and routing, ships growth experiments like website widgets and ROI calculators, and builds data pipelines for attribution.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment with Omri's GTM Engineer target role, AI-native RevOps expertise, and technical marketing operations background. Remote position matches location flexibility, and the role's focus on automation, data pipelines, and RevOps collaboration directly leverages his senior-level capabilities.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines at B2B SaaS, and Pylon's approach to AI-powered outbound automation caught my attention. Your GTM Engineer role sits exactly at the intersection of what I'm looking for: technical RevOps infrastructure, marketing automation at scale, and actually shipping growth experiments. I've owned similar projects around email deliverability, lead scoring pipelines, and API-driven automation sequences—curious if my background might be useful as you scale the platform.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer/B2B research used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange. As GTM Engineer, you'll code, ship, and iterate on the internal tools and automations that make the GTM team effective, replacing manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows. Build lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines that work, onboarding flows that activate customers faster, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work weekly.
score_reason: Strong GTM/RevOps alignment and remote location are ideal, but the role's heavy engineering/coding focus is a notable gap for a RevOps executive whose strength is strategy, data, and process rather than software development. Better suited as a RevOps strategic partner than hands-on GTM Engineer.
ai_opener: Conveo's mission to democratize research resonates—I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines for B2B SaaS using data and automation. Your GTM Engineer role caught my attention: I've designed lead scoring models, attribution systems, and customer activation workflows that eliminate manual work at scale, though traditionally through RevOps stack integration rather than custom code. I'd be curious whether you're open to a RevOps leader who can architect and own these automations end-to-end, even if hands-on engineering isn't my primary toolkit.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Nooks is the AI Sales Assistant Platform that automates busywork so reps can focus on selling, loved by sales teams at HubSpot, Rippling, and hundreds more. As the first GTM Engineer, you'll serve as the technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization, owning setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform. Backed by $70M+ from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins, the company has grown ARR 4x then 3x and plans to 3x again this year.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, AI-native platform expertise, and RevOps background. Nooks' focus on sales enablement and pipeline optimization directly matches Omri's core competencies, though the role is more specialized/tactical than his typical Head of Revenue scope.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15 years building RevOps/GTM functions at B2B SaaS companies, and what Nooks is doing with AI-native sales automation is exactly where the industry is moving. Your 4x→3x→3x growth trajectory tells me you need someone who can bridge technical platform setup with revenue operations strategy, which is where I've consistently added the most value.

description: Prompt builds modern, automated B2B software for rehab therapy businesses.
